But he does it because he wants us to walk in his ways. He doesn't want us to lean on our own understanding. He wants us to trust, to live by faith. For 40 years, children of Israel would have had to follow this process. They would have to go out and gather up the manna. You know, God was faithful to provide. And God's been faithful to us. He's never failed you and he never will. He's never failed me and he never will. And so this evening, we look at this portion of scripture and we see that God says that I may prove them. Will we walk in his ways or no? Will we obey him or no? Tonight, I hope that our choice would be, yes, Lord, yes. I wanna see those details, the meticulous process, every jot and every tittle, and I wanna obey all of it because I want your blessing. I want your blessing. Let's pray. Father, we love you. Thank you for loving us. Thank you for your word. I thank you for the truth contained therein. And as we can look at examples from the Old Testament, we find that Paul said this as he's writing to the church in Corinth, that they were written as in samples to us for our learning, for our admonition. And Lord, I pray that we would take heed to what we read in the Old Testament account of the children of Israel and their behavior, and that we would analyze and evaluate our own lives and determine, are we walking in your way or no? Lord, we know deep down, we know your Holy Spirit convicts us. You're so gracious to deal with us as individuals. You're so long-suffering. And I pray, God, that we would be responsive to you, that we would just determine that we're gonna live according to your word. Bless our time of prayer. We pray that through all that's said and done, that we'd be glorified for it's in Christ's name we pray. Amen.
